'''Manu Maʻu''' (born 24 August 1988) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Souths Sharks]] in [[Mackay, Queensland]]. He has played for both [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Ma'u was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]], and is of [[Tonga]]n descent. He attended [[rugby league]] nursery [[St Paul's College, Auckland]].

He played his junior football for the [[Richmond Rovers]] and [[Marist Saints]].

==Early years==
Ma'u was an original member of Auckland's Roskill South-based "JDK", one of the many street [[gangs in New Zealand]].<ref name="smh"/> In 2007, after one of the gang was stabbed during an altercation, the gang gatecrashed a 21st birthday party in an alcohol-fuelled quest for vengeance, found the alleged perpetrators and "unleashed hell".<ref name="smh"/> Two months later, Maʻu was arrested along with his brother, Semisi. He was convicted of grievous bodily harm with intent to injure and served 22 months in prison.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.1news.co.nz/2017/11/16/watch-hope-and-pray-they-dont-come-back-tongan-star-manu-mau-inspires-young-offenders-to-change/ |title=Watch: 'Hope and pray they don't come back' - Tongan star Manu Ma'u inspires young offenders to change |publisher=1 News |date=16 November 2017 |access-date=31 May 2022}}</ref>

Ma'u was released in 2009 to work and continue his rugby league career,<ref name="smh"/> and joined the [[New Zealand Warriors|New Zealand Warriors']] reserves team the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[New South Wales Cup]]. But he found himself unable to play away games, as the Australian immigration department denied him a visa, citing his criminal history.<ref name="smh">{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-eels-recruit-manu-mau-rises-from-big-house-to-the-big-time-20140308-34e05.html|title=Parramatta Eels recruit Manu Ma'u rises from big house to the big time|date=8 March 2014|publisher=smh.com.au|access-date=2014-03-09}}</ref>

In 2013, after his manager and the [[Parramatta Eels]] battled for a year to gain the necessary visa, Ma'u moved to Australia to play in the [[New South Wales Cup]] for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]], the Eels' reserves team.
[[File: manumau.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Ma'u playing for Parramatta in 2013]]
A reformed man with a young family of his own, Ma'u was eager to put his past behind him and make the most of his opportunity with the Eels. Ma'u said "I want to repay Parramatta for their faith," "I want to build a future. I want my partner Alisi and my kids [Melino and Levi] to be proud of me and I want my family to realise I'm not that naughty boy that caused them pain."<ref name="stuff">{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/9832806/Rising-star-Manus-free-fairytale|title=Rising Eels star Manu Ma'u's free fairytale | Stuff.co.nz|date=17 March 2014|publisher=stuff.co.nz|access-date=2014-03-30}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2014===
In February 2014, Ma'u was included in the Eels inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2015-12-27|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=2016-01-15}}</ref> In Round 1 of the [[2014 NRL season]], Ma'u made his NRL debut for the [[Parramatta Eels]] against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at {{rlp|sr}}. Ma’u won the Man of the Match award on debut in Parramatta's 36-16 win at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref name="parraeels">{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/03/09/eels_vs_warriors_late_mail.html|title=Eels vs Warriors Late Mail - Eels|date=9 March 2014|publisher=parraeels.com.au|access-date=2014-03-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/eels-thrilled-with-mau-debut/tabid/10874/newsid/76686/default.aspx|title=Eels thrilled with Ma'u debut|website=NRL.com|date=2014-03-09|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> In Round 4 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Ma’u scored his first NRL career try in the 32-16 win at Parramatta Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|author=Adrian Proszenko|url=http://www.theage.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/parramatta-eels-beat-penrith-panthers-3216-20140329-zqohs.html|title=Parramatta Eels beat Penrith Panthers 32-16|website=Theage.com.au|date=2014-03-29|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> On 5 June 2014, Ma’u extended his contract with the Parramatta club for a further three years to the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-safeguard-their-future-after-resigning-manu-mau-kenny-edwards-and-vai-toutai/story-fni3ga7r-1226944524182|title=Parramatta Eels Safeguard Future After Re-Signing Manu Mau, Kenny Edwards and Vai Toutai|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2014-03-29|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> In Round 14 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] at [[ANZ Stadium]], Ma’u debut year in the NRL was over after suffering a fractured forearm in the Parramatta's 22-12 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-overcome-jarryd-hayne-absence-with-classy-victory-over-canterbury-bulldogs/story-fni3fbgz-1226954977680|title=Parramatta Eels Overcome Jarryd Hayne Absence With Classy Victory Over Canterbury Bulldogs|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2014-03-29|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> Ma’u finished off his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 13 matches and scoring a try for the Parramatta side in the 2014 NRL season.

===2015===
On 31 January and 1 February, Ma'u played for the Eels in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-2015-nines-squad/|title=EELS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-26|access-date=2015-12-27|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150707105149/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=2015-07-07|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 2 May, Ma'u represented [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in their 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]] test-match against Pacific rivals [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], playing at second-row in Tonga's 18-16 loss at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/video-sam-kasiano-try-saving-tackle-on-jorge-taufua-secures-pacific-test-win-for-samoan-against-tonga/story-e6frf3ou-1227331678892|title=Sam Kasiano Try Saving Tackle on Jorge Taufua Secures Pacific Test Win for Samoa Against Tonga|website=Foxsports.com.au|date=2014-03-29|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> He finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 22 matches and scoring 5 tries for Parramatta club.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDcxNi0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-11-01|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> On 8 September, he was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] train-on squad,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/league/news/article.cfm?c_id=79&objectid=11509658|title=League: Kiwis to manage Mannering's workload on England tour|work=The New Zealand Herald}}</ref> however didn't make the final squad so played for Tonga in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]], playing at second-row in Tonga's 28-8 win at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/tonga-qualify-for-2017-world-cup/tabid/10874/newsid/90566/default.aspx|title=Tonga qualify for 2017 World Cup|website=NRL.com|date=2015-10-17|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref>

===2016===
On 1 February 2016, Ma'u was named in the Eels' [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-name-2016-nines-squad/|title=Eels name 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|access-date=2016-02-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204104458/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/eels-name-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 6 May 2016, Ma'u made his debut for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2016 Anzac Test]], starting at second-row in the 16-0 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|title=UPDATED: Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-05-07|archive-date=4 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160604182259/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/79684243/from-watching-the-kiwis-in-prison-to-making-his-nz-debut--manu-maus-remarkable-transformation|title=From watching the Kiwis in prison to making NZ debut for Manu Ma'u|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|date=2014-01-23|access-date=2016-05-07}}</ref> On 15 September 2016 at the Parramatta presentation night, Ma’u was awarded with the Ken Thornett Medal as being the club's Players’ Player of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2016/09/15/manu_ma_u_claims_ken.af_news.html|title=Manu Ma'u claims Ken Thornett Medal|website=Parreels.com.au|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/your-clubs-2016-player-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/102002/default.aspx|title=Your club's 2016 player of the year|website=Nrl.com|date=9 October 2016|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref> Ma’u finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches and scoring two tries for the Parramatta club. On 25 September 2016, Ma’u was rewarded for his solid season with the club by getting his contract extended to the end of the 2019 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/private-ryan-dragons-luciano-leiluas-deciding-factor-bad-boy-kirisome-auvaa-gets-help/news-story/d7f3def1d7ecf9f1134006fad561a644|title=NRL 2016: Manu Ma'u re-signs with Eels, Dragons' Luciano Leilua — Private Ryan|date=25 September 2016|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref> On 4 October 2016, Ma’u was selected in the New Zealand Kiwis 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/four-nations/four-nations-new-zealand-kiwis-coach-david-kidwell-names-his-squad-for-end-of-season-uk-tour/news-story/071d098ece166747c24c06befde4aee9|title=Four Nations: New Zealand Kiwis David Kidwell names rookies Jordan Rapana, Joseph Tapine|date=3 October 2016|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref> Ma’u played in all 5 matches of the tournament including starting at second-row in the Kiwis 34-8 Four Nations Final loss against Australia at [[Anfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/86610155/fast-start-the-key-for-kiwis-in-four-nations-final-says-manu-mau|title=Fast start the key for Kiwis in Four Nations final, says Manu Ma'u|website=Stuff|date=18 November 2016|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/four-nations/live-four-nations-final-australian-kangaroos-v-new-zealand-kiwis-scores-blog-updates/news-story/6c7b883d27ab040752cab13db9a6045b|title=Four Nations rugby league: Australia Kangaroos beat New Zealand Kiwis live scores, updates, live blog, news|date=20 November 2016|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=5 October 2017}}</ref>

===2017===
In the 2017 season, Ma'u played 24 games for Parramatta as they finished fourth on the table at the end of the season and qualified for the finals which was the club's first appearance since the 2009 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/market-watch-transfer-chat-eels-bargain-basement-gamble-thats-paid-off-big-time/news-story/6851bcc4ae2855f3c56aeef63873f8a2|title = Market Watch Transfer Chat: Eels' bargain basement gamble that's paid off big time|date = 14 September 2017}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjk2LTQtLS0tLS0yMDcxNi0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title = Custom Match List - Rugby League Project}}</ref>

Ma'u elected to switch his national allegiance [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in 2017.  
[[File:OSTIIICeremony3.jpg|thumb|right|Ma'u representing Tonga in 2017]]
He was named in the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] and played in every match of their historic charge to the semi-finals.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/mark-geyer-says-jason-taumalolo-has-changed-the-world-cup-forever/news-story/0708c55290b3d8d36727d7a453431a31|title = Call will change rugby league 'forever'|newspaper = News.com.au|date = 27 November 2017}}</ref> He scored a try in their victory over arch-rivals [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].

===2018===
On 27 June 2018, Ma'u was ruled out for the rest of the season after suffering a fractured cheekbone in a middle of the season test match while playing for Tonga against Samoa.  Even though Parramatta were running last at the time, Ma'u was cited as being one of the better players in the struggling side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/brad-arthur-has-given-his-players-nine-games-to-prove-they-deserve-to-keep-their-spots-at-the-parramatta-eels/news-story/8bcc362b8dc3595bff0122b8085d7bd8|title=Brad Arthur has given his players nine games to prove they deserve to keep their spots at the Parramatta Eels|date=27 June 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
After missing the start of the season through injury, Ma'u made his return for Parramatta in Round 7 against Newcastle scoring a try in a 28-14 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/6093892/knights-show-true-colours-in-much-needed-win-against-parra/|title=Newcastle Knights show true colours in much-needed win against Parra|website=www.theherald.com.au}}</ref>
On 8 July 2019, it was announced that Ma'u had signed a 2-year deal with [[Hull F.C.]] in England starting in the 2020 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48912905|title=Manu Ma'u: Parramatta Eels back-rower joins Super League side Hull FC from 2020|website=BBC}}</ref>

At the end of the 2019 regular season, Parramatta finished in 5th place on the table and qualified for the finals.  In the elimination final against Brisbane, Ma'u kicked a goal in his last home game as Parramatta won the match 58-0 at the new [[Western Sydney Stadium]].  The victory was the biggest finals win in history, eclipsing Newtown's 55-7 win over St George in 1944.  The match was also Parramatta's biggest win over Brisbane and Brisbane's worst ever loss since entering the competition in 1988.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-09-15/eels-make-history-with-58-point-finals-flogging-of-broncos/11505120/|title=Parramatta Eels eliminate Brisbane Broncos from the NRL Finals with a thumping 58-0 win|newspaper=ABC News|date=15 September 2019}}</ref>

Ma'u played his final game for Parramatta the following week as the club were defeated 32-0 by Melbourne in the elimination semi final at [[AAMI Park]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-09-21/nrl-live-semi-final-melbourne-storm-parramatta-eels/11527200/|title=Melbourne Storm crush Parramatta 32-0 in their semi-final to set up prelim against the Roosters|newspaper=ABC News|date=21 September 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
He played 11 games for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 2020 Super League season including the semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
Ma'u played 20 games for Hull F.C. in the 2021 Super League season in which the club finished 8th on the table and missed out on the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415217/terry-oconnors-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Terry O'Connor's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2022===
On 12 July 2022, Ma'u signed a two-year deal to join the [[Catalans Dragons]] starting in 2023. Mau'u played a total of 14 games for Hull F.C. in the 2022 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-fcs-manu-mau-makes-big-super-league-move-55934/|title=Hull FC's Manu Ma’u makes big Super League move|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 4 of the 2023 Super League season, Ma'u scored two tries and was also sin binned during Catalans 18-10 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64907127|title=Wigan Warriors 10-18 Catalans Dragons - Les Dracs maintain unbeaten run|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 14 October, Ma'u played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
Ma'u would depart Catalans at the end of the season. It was announced on 9 November that Ma'u had returned to Australia and signed with Souths Sharks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=2024-11-09 |title=Former Hull FC star Manu Ma'u signs for new club |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/former-hull-fc-star-manu-mau-signs-for-new-club-98971/ |access-date=2024-11-16 |website=Serious About Rugby League |language=en-GB}}</ref>
